title: tensor_embedding_backward is a row-0-only stub — gradients past row 0 are dropped
goal: Embedding lookup forward is y[i,:] = W[idx[i],:]; the correct backward scatters dL/dy rows into dL/dW at idx[i]. tensor_embedding_backward (lib/bridge/tensor_backward.cpp) doesn't have the lookup-index tensor wired through the ad_node_t structure yet, so it falls back to accumulating every row's gradient into row 0 of the weight tensor. Training any model with embeddings indexed at rows other than 0 will not converge correctly. A one-shot stderr warning already fires on first invocation (so this cannot regress silently), and docs/breakdown/AUTODIFF.md documents the limitation. Previously the code/docs claimed this would ship 'in v1.3' without a tracked ticket backing that claim; both have now been corrected to reference this ticket instead of a version promise.
next_actions: Thread the lookup-index tensor through node->input2 (or an equivalent new field) at the point the embedding forward op is recorded onto the AD tape, per the comment already left in tensor_embedding_backward.
Implement the real indexed scatter: dW[idx[i]] += dy[i] for each row i, replacing the row-0 accumulation fallback.
Add tests/autodiff/embedding_backward_test.esk with a multi-row lookup (indices hitting rows 0, 3, 7 of a small embedding table) and assert each row's gradient lands correctly, not just row 0.
Remove the one-shot stderr warning once the real scatter ships; update docs/breakdown/AUTODIFF.md's Embedding backward section to describe the completed implementation.
acceptance: Training a model with embedding lookups against non-zero rows converges (gradient reaches the correct rows, not just row 0).
The stderr stub warning is removed once fixed (its continued presence is itself evidence this ticket is still open).
ux_payoff: Unblocks correct training of any embedding-based model (the standard first layer of virtually every NLP/recommendation architecture) — currently a silent correctness trap for anyone using embeddings beyond row 0.
